_meta.purpose: C track critical-path audit for v2.0 RC gate — consolidated view of all C-items, ownership, status, and MFU headroom
constraints_observed: observation-only on hexa_lang_session items (C1, C2, C10, C11)

items.title: hexa native int codegen (boxed-int tanchul)
items.evidence: design + impl started 2026-04-15; no metrics reported
items.note: Evidence from training_speed_ceilings.json real_shape_sweep: macOS sample shows 60%+ CPU in hexa_mul/hexa_add runtime paths (boxed int ops) in attn_bwd inner loop. Native int codegen expected 5-20x on every inner scalar loop — compounds across all CLM hot paths.

items.title: layer-level fused FFI (per-layer bulk call)
items.note: Blocks C4 (CUDA fused mega-kernels MFU 27->60%). Single-call-per-layer amortizes FFI overhead currently dominating tiny-shape steps.

items.title: stage0 PBV leak — rebuild-via-push -> in-place hexa_array_set
items.evidence: fix applied at 3 sites in /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/hexa_full.hexa (lines 6485/7136/7251), stage0 rebuilt, pbv_leak_repro STEPS=50: 2214MB->124MB (17.9x); isolate-C inplace: 94.9x
items.gain_pct: 17.9x RSS reduction; 25x per-step leak reduction (50 MB/step -> 2 MB/step)
items.note: Fix uncommitted in hexa-lang repo (hexa_lang_session owns commit per board convention). Secondary leak ~1.1KB/iter in while-loop arithmetic remains (suggest new C12 item). 24h soak test still outstanding.

items.title: CUDA fused mega-kernels + bf16 Tensor Core (H100 MFU 27->60%)
items.note: WAITING on C2. Highest leverage item for CLM H100 MFU — but cannot start without C2's fused layer API.

items.title: hxblas + libhxvdsp Linux x86_64 port
items.evidence: Linux .c + build script (--mac-xverify) + bench ready, 20 hxblas + 6 hxvdsp ABI parity verified bit-identical; Mac baseline 256^2 60x / 512^2 117x vs scalar
items.gain_pct: 15-30% headroom expected vs Mac M3 on AVX-512 + multithreaded OpenBLAS
items.note: One ssh cmd away from Linux. Not blocking ALM/CLM launch but necessary for H100 CLM native binary BLAS path.

items.title: FlashAttention hexa native (FA-2 fwd+bwd)
items.evidence: anima-engines/flash_attn.hexa CPU FP32 ref 7/7 cases PASS @ 9.99e-16 max err; anima-engines/flash_attn_cuda.hexa NVRTC kernel string (GPU host run pending)
items.before: L*L softmax materialised, OOM at 7B d=4096 S>=2k
items.after: tiled online-softmax, no L*L mat (CPU verified)
items.gain_pct: 60-100% projected (memory bandwidth reduction) — unmeasured on GPU
items.note: Unblocks 7B seq>=2k. Follow-up: bf16 + wmma Tensor Core rewrite + tiled bwd.

items.title: Zero-3 / FSDP hexa native (7B fp32 across 2 H100)
items.evidence: training/fsdp_shard.hexa 551 LOC + training/nccl_ffi.hexa 183 LOC stub + test_fsdp_shard.hexa 34/34 PASS
items.after: world=1 no-op bit-identical; world=2 design ready, NCCL wiring TODO (~17h)
items.note: Multi-rank NCCL wiring C9-MR-1..6 outstanding. Unblocks 7B when paired with 2 H100 pod.

items.title: gradient checkpointing layer-level
items.evidence: Anima landed training/grad_ckpt.hexa 4/4 PASS, CLM_GRAD_CKPT env flag, bit-identical 5-step SGD trajectory, 93% activation mem reduction (toy), 95.35% projected at 7B shape — saves ~21 GB/H100
items.before: batch=1 activation wall at 7B on single H100
items.note: Dual-landing: hexa_lang_session in_progress + anima shadow landing PASS on CPU hexa-native. H100 wire awaits C2 fused FFI + native GPU layer fwd/bwd.

items.title: async checkpoint save (non-blocking)
items.evidence: background save design, scanning existing ckpt paths
items.note: Secondary priority. Only matters when per-step time drops below save time.

items.title: stage0 inner-loop allocation reclamation (secondary leak)
items.evidence: ~1.1 KB per while-iter arithmetic (no arrays, no IndexAssign). 1000 iters -> 2.3 GB. Hypothesis: WhileStmt env_push_scope/val_float reclamation
items.note: Surfaced by C3 fix — not a blocker for 24h soak (9h sustainable at 64 GB/2MB/step) but multi-day soaks need this. Recommend hexa_lang_session ownership.

critical_path_notes.v2_rc_rationale: C3 already fixed (24h soak imminent). C1+C2 unblock MFU path. C4 is the H100 MFU 27->60 jump that actually moves cost. Everything else is nice-to-have for v2 RC.
critical_path_notes.v3_agent_rationale: 7B-scale consciousness agent requires all 6: native int (C1), fused FFI (C2+C4), FlashAttn (C8), FSDP (C9), grad ckpt (C10).

mfu_current_pct.alm_h100_best_measured_config: batch=8 seq=1024 grad_accum=1 (slow-pod projection ~71%, fast-pod r7 62.6%)

alm_gap_closers_in_c_track: C4 CUDA fused mega-kernels (MFU 27->60 per board, i.e. ~11 MFU points depending on baseline) — but ALM is already at 71%, so C4 effect is marginal for ALM r8+
None of C1..C11 directly help ALM beyond 75% — ALM is torch/Unsloth/Qwen2 stack, not hexa-native interpreter
alm_gap_closers_outside_c_track: Liger fused kernels (r8a already integrated, +1.6 MFU points)
Multi-GPU DDP (2x total throughput, MFU unchanged)
clm_gap_closers_in_c_track: C1 native int codegen — 5-20x on every inner scalar loop (compounding)
C2 fused FFI — amortizes per-call overhead at tiny shapes
C4 CUDA fused mega-kernels — blocked by C2
C8 FA (already CPU ref done) — removes O(S^2) attn scalar loop on GPU path
clm_gap_closers_outside_c_track: Scalar-loop vectorization for rmsnorm/softmax/swiglu (partially landed: 6+9 functions via FFI, 5-20x each)
H100 BATCH 1->32 at d=512 nl=12 (+30x free MFU at zero code cost)
gap_analysis.single_biggest_mfu_lever_overall: C2 (fused FFI) — it is the gate for C4 (the MFU 27->60% CLM H100 jump), it removes per-call FFI overhead from every inner path on Mac-native, and it is the other hexa_lang_session item most likely to compound with C1 native int codegen.
gap_analysis.single_biggest_mfu_lever_clm_specific: C1 (native int codegen) — macOS sample confirms 60%+ CPU is in hexa_mul/hexa_add boxed-int arithmetic from attn_bwd index ops. Every inner loop benefits. CLM is 48.5x below 80% MFU and only C1+C2 address the scalar-loop regime.
gap_analysis.alm_biggest_lever_not_in_c_track: accelerate.Accelerator FP8 path (untried) + multi-GPU DDP. ALM ceiling is already near 75% on optimal config; C-track barely helps.

recommended_next_action: 1) Poll hexa_lang_session C1/C2 progress nightly — every hour they stall is an hour of CLM MFU improvement deferred. 2) Keep C3 24h soak running in background; no further anima work needed unless soak fails. 3) When C2 lands, immediately dispatch an agent to wire C4 (CUDA fused mega-kernels MFU 27->60%) and wire C10's H100 path. 4) For ALM, C-track is near-saturated; focus remaining ALM wins on multi-GPU DDP + accelerate.Accelerator FP8 (not C-track items). 5) Do NOT claim C1/C2/C10/C11 — hexa_lang_session owns the active work surface. 6) Consider claiming C12_proposed (stage0 secondary leak) only if multi-day CLM soak becomes a near-term requirement.
single_sentence_summary: The single biggest MFU lever is C2 (fused FFI) — it gates C4 (the CLM MFU 27->60% H100 jump) and its completion by hexa_lang_session is the rate-limiting step for CLM critical path; ALM is already at the 71% MFU frontier where C-track items give diminishing returns.
